title: Separate Preview and Program with an explicit Take workflow

claims.statement: Scene activation, variable changes, and the embedded Panel currently mutate one shared channel that the Studio labels as Program.
The OBS renderer currently subscribes to the same channel state used by interactive Studio clients, so there is no runtime boundary between preparation and on-air output.
Saved Scenes are stored independently from the ephemeral channel runtime and can be copied without modifying their persisted source record.
A Show-scoped production state machine can establish Preview and Program without changing the saved Scene schema or exposing transport channel identifiers in Studio.
Ephemeral in-process production snapshots are sufficient for this phase provided reconnects recover the current state; durable recovery after a server restart requires a separate governed change.
claims.evidence: SPEC-0001#REQ-RUN-001 requires reconnect snapshots but does not require restart persistence

claims.statement: Typed component controls and explicit direct-to-Program mode remain unimplemented and are not claimed by this change.

successCriteria.statement: Each Show has independently observable Preview and Program snapshots with separate monotonic revisions, and a reconnect receives the current snapshot for its selected bus.
successCriteria.verification: Production state-machine tests and WebSocket subscription tests.

successCriteria.statement: Loading a saved Scene or an Editor payload replaces Preview while Program and the persisted Scene remain byte-equivalent to their prior state.
successCriteria.verification: Route and state-machine tests compare Program and saved Scene values before and after Preview loads.

successCriteria.statement: Take validates the expected Preview revision, atomically copies the complete Preview snapshot to Program, is idempotent by operation identifier, and acknowledges connected operators on both buses.
successCriteria.verification: State-machine, conflict, idempotency, and WebSocket broadcast tests.

successCriteria.statement: Output-token WebSocket clients may subscribe to Program but are denied Preview, while Studio sessions may inspect either bus.
Studio Production presents a Scene rundown, Preview and Program monitors, revisions, and an explicit Take command without exposing channel identifiers.
successCriteria.verification: Studio unit tests and desktop/mobile browser inspection.

successCriteria.statement: Editor Send to Preview targets the Show Preview bus, and generated OBS URLs always target that Show's Program bus.
successCriteria.verification: Editor request tests, URL contract tests, and an authenticated Editor to Preview to Take to OBS browser flow.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0008 references ADR-0004 and SPEC-0001 and its final implemented record contains no blocking unknown.
Production state, revisions, source isolation, expected-revision conflicts, Take idempotency, and output authorization have automated tests.
Studio Production and Output surfaces are verified at desktop and mobile sizes, and the live Program output changes only after Take.
Documentation describes Preview, Program, Take, ephemeral restart behavior, and Program-only OBS URLs without claiming typed controls or device support.
The complete governance, quality, test, build, and dependency audit suites pass for the current manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check and npm run check and npm run build and npm audit

definitionOfDone.statement: The signed pull-request commit and GitHub observation are current for the final planHash and manifestHash.
